rationale: The cited paper (Berns 2005) is used only once in the introduction to support a narrow background point that drawing attention to marketing claims can raise performance beyond baseline. The citing article's core framing, hypotheses, and empirical extension are built around Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely (2005), not around Berns's commentary. There is no further use of Berns's conceptual or neural account in the method, analysis, or discussion, so the citation functions as a passing contextual mention.
